steps.title: Find the median position
steps.content:
Each data set has $11$ values. For $11$ values, the median is the middle value:
$$\frac{11+1}{2}=6$$
So compare the 6th value in each sorted list.

steps.title: Check data set A
steps.content:
Data set A in order is:
$$2,2,2,5,5,8,11,11,14,14,14$$
The 6th value is $8$, so the median of data set A is $8$.

steps.title: Check data set B
steps.content:
Data set B in order is:
$$2,5,5,8,8,8,8,8,11,11,14$$
The 6th value is also $8$, so the median of data set B is $8$.

steps.title: Compare the medians
steps.content: Both medians are $8$, so the medians are equal. That matches choice C, so C is correct.
